---
title: "Packing List"
subtitle: "What to Bring to Italy in June"
date: "2026-06-04"
categories: [packing, planning, logistics]
toc: true
---

## June in Italy: What to Expect

**Florence:** High 80s°F (30°C), sunny, occasional afternoon thunderstorms
**Naples:** High 80s-90s°F (30-32°C), humid, hot
**Puglia:** High 80s°F (30°C), coastal breezes, sunny

**Pack for:** Heat, walking, cobblestones, church dress codes, varied elevations

---

## Luggage Strategy

### Checked Bag

**1 checked bag per person** (max 50 lbs/23 kg for economy)

**What Goes Here:**
- Most clothes
- Toiletries over 3.4 oz
- Shoes (except what you're wearing)
- Souvenirs on return

### Carry-On

**1 carry-on + 1 personal item**

**Carry-On Bag Contains:**
- 1-2 changes of clothes (in case checked bag delayed)
- Medications
- Valuables (jewelry, electronics)
- Travel documents
- Phone chargers
- Essentials for first 24 hours

**Personal Item (Backpack/Purse):**
- Passport, wallet, phone
- Snacks, water bottle (empty through security)
- Book, headphones
- Sweater/light jacket

---

## Clothing

### Tops (Pack 5-7)

- [ ] 3-4 Short-sleeve shirts (breathable fabrics)
- [ ] 1-2 Long-sleeve shirts (sun protection, churches)
- [ ] 1 Light cardigan or jacket (AC in museums, trains)
- [ ] 1 Nicer top for dinners

**Fabrics:** Linen, cotton, moisture-wicking (avoid synthetic in heat)

### Bottoms (Pack 3-4)

- [ ] 2 Pairs comfortable pants/jeans (NOT too tight or low-cut for churches)
- [ ] 1 Pair shorts (knee-length for church dress code)
- [ ] 1 Skirt or dress (if preferred)

**Church Dress Code:** Shoulders and knees covered (carry scarf to cover up)

### Dresses/Skirts (Optional, 2-3)

- [ ] Sundresses (with scarf to cover shoulders in churches)
- [ ] Casual skirt (knee-length or longer)

### Outerwear

- [ ] 1 Light rain jacket (packable, also windbreaker)
- [ ] 1 Sweater or light jacket for AC

### Undergarments & Sleepwear

- [ ] 7-8 Underwear
- [ ] 2-3 Bras (including 1 sports bra for walking)
- [ ] 7-8 Pairs socks (light, moisture-wicking)
- [ ] Pajamas/sleepwear

### Accessories

- [ ] Sun hat or cap (wide brim for sun protection)
- [ ] Sunglasses
- [ ] Scarf (covers shoulders for churches, stylish, warmth on plane)
- [ ] Belt
- [ ] Swimsuit (hotel pools, maybe Puglia beaches)

---

## Footwear (Most Important!)

### Walking Shoes (Pack 2 Pairs)

**Priority #1:** You'll walk 5-10 miles per day on cobblestones

**Option 1:** Broken-in sneakers or walking shoes
- MUST be already broken in (no blisters!)
- Good arch support
- Cushioned sole
- Examples: Allbirds, Hoka, Brooks, New Balance

**Option 2:** Comfortable sandals with support
- NOT flip-flops (no support)
- Closed-toe for cobblestones
- Examples: Birkenstocks, Teva, Keen

**Rule:** Alternate shoes daily (give feet/shoes rest)

### Nicer Shoes (1 Pair)

- [ ] Comfortable flats or low heels for dinners
- MUST still be walkable (you'll walk to restaurant)
- Avoid stilettos on cobblestones

**Pro Tip:** Break in ALL shoes 2 months before trip!

---

## Toiletries

### TSA-Compliant (3.4 oz / 100ml for carry-on)

- [ ] Toothbrush & toothpaste
- [ ] Deodorant
- [ ] Shampoo & conditioner (or buy there)
- [ ] Face wash
- [ ] Moisturizer (with SPF for daytime)
- [ ] Razor
- [ ] Contact lens solution (if applicable)
- [ ] Makeup (minimal - it's hot)

### Sun Protection (Essential!)

- [ ] **Sunscreen SPF 30+** (apply daily, reapply)
- [ ] Lip balm with SPF
- [ ] After-sun lotion (aloe)

### Medications

- [ ] Prescription meds (2-week supply + extra)
- [ ] Pain reliever (ibuprofen/acetaminophen)
- [ ] Antihistamine (allergies)
- [ ] Anti-diarrheal (just in case)
- [ ] Band-aids & blister prevention
- [ ] Any personal medications

**Bring in original bottles with prescriptions!**

### First Aid

- [ ] Band-aids (for blisters!)
- [ ] Moleskin or Compeed (blister prevention/treatment)
- [ ] Antibiotic ointment
- [ ] Tweezers
- [ ] Pain relief patches

---

## Electronics

### Essential

- [ ] **Phone** (unlocked for international SIM or with int'l plan)
- [ ] Phone charger
- [ ] Portable battery pack
- [ ] **Power adapter (Type C, F, or L plugs for Italy)**
- [ ] Headphones
- [ ] Camera (if not using phone)
- [ ] E-reader or tablet

### Optional

- [ ] Laptop (if needed for work)
- [ ] Universal power strip (charge multiple devices)
- [ ] Selfie stick (if you use)

**Note:** Most hotels have 1-2 outlets. Bring power strip if multiple devices.

---

## Travel Documents

### Must Have (Carry-On Only!)

- [ ] **Passport** (valid 6+ months beyond return date)
- [ ] Printed flight confirmations
- [ ] Printed hotel confirmations
- [ ] Printed train tickets / museum reservations
- [ ] Travel insurance documents
- [ ] Credit cards (2-3, notify banks of travel dates)
- [ ] Debit card (ATM withdrawals for cash)
- [ ] Driver's license (as backup ID)
- [ ] Emergency contact list
- [ ] Copy of itinerary

**Pro Tip:** Email yourself scans of passport, credit cards, confirmations as backup.

---

## Money

### Cash

- [ ] €200-300 in small bills (€10, €20, €50)
- Get from bank before leaving or ATM at airport
- Avoid currency exchange kiosks (high fees)

### Cards

- [ ] 2-3 Credit cards (Visa/Mastercard widely accepted)
- [ ] 1 Debit card (ATM withdrawals)
- Notify banks of travel dates to avoid fraud holds

**Tip:** Many Italian establishments cash-only or €10 minimum for cards

---

## Bags & Organization

### Day Bag

- [ ] **Crossbody bag or backpack** (hands-free, anti-theft)
- Must fit: wallet, phone, water, sunscreen, guidebook
- Zippers (not magnets) for pickpocket protection

**Recommended:** Anti-theft travel purse (slash-proof, RFID blocking)

### Packing Cubes

- [ ] Packing cubes (organize clothes, compress)
- [ ] Laundry bag (dirty clothes)
- [ ] Shoe bags

### Plastic Bags

- [ ] Gallon Ziplocs (wet clothes, toiletries, leaky items)
- [ ] Grocery bags (dirty shoes, wet swimsuit)

---

## Miscellaneous

### Helpful Items

- [ ] Reusable water bottle (refill at fountains)
- [ ] Small umbrella (June thunderstorms)
- [ ] Earplugs (noisy hotels, especially Naples)
- [ ] Eye mask (for sleeping)
- [ ] Travel pillow (for planes/trains)
- [ ] Guidebook or maps (downloaded offline)
- [ ] Small notebook & pen
- [ ] Tissues/hand wipes
- [ ] Hand sanitizer
- [ ] Snacks (granola bars for long train rides)
- [ ] Laundry detergent packets (sink washing)
- [ ] Clothesline or clips (hang dry clothes)

### Optional But Nice

- [ ] Binoculars (for art details, views)
- [ ] Foldable tote bag (for market purchases, souvenirs)
- [ ] Italian phrasebook or app
- [ ] Travel journal
- [ ] Playing cards

---

## What NOT to Pack

### Leave at Home

- ❌ Hairdryer (hotels provide, heavy)
- ❌ Full-size toiletries (buy there or use travel size)
- ❌ Too many shoes (max 3 pairs)
- ❌ Formal clothes (not needed unless specific event)
- ❌ Heavy books (use e-reader)
- ❌ Expensive jewelry (risk of loss/theft)
- ❌ Too many "just in case" items

**Rule of Thumb:** If unsure, leave it. You can buy almost anything in Italy.

---

## Packing Strategy

### 2 Weeks Before

- [ ] Lay out everything you think you'll bring
- [ ] Try on all clothes (make sure they fit, comfortable)
- [ ] Break in new shoes
- [ ] Test all electronics (charge, ensure working)

### 1 Week Before

- [ ] Remove 1/3 of what you laid out (you packed too much)
- [ ] Check weather forecast
- [ ] Do laundry of items you're bringing
- [ ] Gather toiletries

### 3 Days Before

- [ ] Pack non-essentials in checked bag
- [ ] Make sure medications are accessible
- [ ] Charge all electronics
- [ ] Print documents

### Day Before

- [ ] Pack carry-on
- [ ] Weigh bags (checked max 50 lbs)
- [ ] Set aside travel outfit
- [ ] Double-check passport, tickets, confirmations

### Day Of

- [ ] Wear heaviest shoes (save weight in luggage)
- [ ] Layer clothes on plane (adjust to temperature)
- [ ] Keep valuables in personal item

---

## Special Considerations

### Church Dress Code

**Required:** Shoulders and knees covered

**Solutions:**
- Carry lightweight scarf (covers shoulders instantly)
- Wear knee-length shorts/skirt
- Long pants always acceptable
- Some churches provide wraps (but not all)

**They WILL turn you away** if improperly dressed at major churches/museums.

### Walking on Cobblestones

- Broken-in shoes with support
- Avoid thin soles, high heels, flip-flops
- Bring moleskin for blisters
- Expect sore feet - it's normal!

### Heat Management

- Light colors (reflect sun)
- Breathable fabrics (linen, cotton)
- Sunscreen (SPF 30+ daily)
- Hat and sunglasses
- Refillable water bottle

---

## Final Checklist

### 3 Days Before Departure

- [ ] Check passport validity
- [ ] Print all confirmations
- [ ] Notify credit card companies
- [ ] Download offline maps
- [ ] Pack medications
- [ ] Charge all devices
- [ ] Set up international phone plan or buy SIM

### Day of Departure

- [ ] Passport in carry-on
- [ ] Boarding pass (digital or printed)
- [ ] Empty water bottle (refill after security)
- [ ] Snacks for plane
- [ ] Entertainment (book, music, podcast)
- [ ] Comfortable travel outfit

---

**Buon viaggio! Pack light, walk far, eat well.** 🇮🇹
